$comment: DRAFT manifest for the .mcpb MCP Bundle (Claude Desktop Extension). Validate with `mcpb validate` before packing; field set follows github.com/modelcontextprotocol/mcpb as of 2026-06. Do not publish before the repo is public (approval gate).
name: servomotor-mcp
description: Drive Gearotons M17 open-source servomotors from plain English over RS-485 — moves, homing, sequences, telemetry. A thin control layer with full range of motion.
long_description: The M17 is an open-source, closed-loop NEMA-17 integrated servomotor (motor + driver + controller + encoder in one, daisy-chainable over RS-485). This server exposes 9 tools so Claude can move real hardware: verified on production motors at 0.000° closed-loop repeatability, with full multi-turn range (the server forwards commands straight to the motor; the motor's firmware self-protects against over-current/voltage/temperature). A mock backend lets you try everything with no hardware attached.

backend.title: Backend
backend.description: 'mock' to try without hardware; 'serial' to drive real motors over RS-485.

serial_port.title: Serial port
serial_port.description: RS-485 adapter port (e.g. /dev/cu.usbserial-210 on macOS, COM3 on Windows). Ignored by the mock backend.

tools.name: list_motors
tools.description: List configured motors, aliases, and limits

tools.name: move_to
tools.description: Move a motor to an absolute angle (degrees), safety-clamped

tools.name: move_relative
tools.description: Move a motor by a relative angle, safety-clamped

tools.name: trapezoid_move
tools.description: Timed trapezoidal move to an angle over a duration

tools.name: home
tools.description: Home one or all motors (zero reference)

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Position/telemetry for one or all motors

tools.name: stop
tools.description: Stop one or all motors immediately

tools.name: reset
tools.description: Firmware system reset to recover from faults

tools.name: run_sequence
tools.description: Run a list of move/wait steps as one sequence
